Interface ITree

Represents a control that displays a hierarchical list of tree nodes.

The ITree interface is implemented by the UI library and is not intended to be implemented by consumer code.

Inherited Members
ISynchronizeInvoke.BeginInvoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.EndInvoke(IAsyncResult)
ISynchronizeInvoke.Invoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.InvokeRequired
IWin32Window.Handle
IComponent.Site
IComponent.Disposed
IDisposable.IsDisposed
IDisposable.Dispose()
Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public interface ITree : ISynchronizeInvoke, IWin32Window, IComponent, IDisposable, IDisposable
Remarks

Instances of ITree are created using Create(ITreeEventHandlers).

The lifetime of an ITree instance is managed by the consumer. The consumer is responsible for disposing the tree when it is no longer needed.

The tree displays data using nodes. Use the Append(string, object) method to add a new node to the tree.

The RootNodes property provides access to nodes at the tree’s root level. Each node has an Nodes collection that stores its child nodes.

The following API can be used to retrieve nodes within the tree:

  • FocusedNode – Gets or sets the currently focused node.
  • NodeSelection – Gets the collection of selected nodes.

Properties

CustomSort

Gets or sets a custom comparer used to sort ITreeNode objects.

Declaration
IComparer<ITreeNode> CustomSort { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
IComparer<ITreeNode>
Remarks

Assign an implementation of IComparer<T> to define custom node sorting logic.

public class CustomNodeComparer : IComparer<ITreeNode>
{
    public int Compare(ITreeNode x, ITreeNode y)
    {
        return string.Compare(x.Text, y.Text);
    }
}

tree.CustomSort = new CustomNodeComparer();

Assign null to restore the default sorting behavior.

FocusedNode

Gets or sets the node that currently has input focus within the tree.

Declaration
ITreeNode FocusedNode { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITreeNode
Remarks

The FocusedNode property allows you to retrieve or change the node that currently has focus. When the focused node changes, the corresponding focus-change notification is raised.

If an invisible node (see Visible) is assigned to this property, the value is reset to null.

Exceptions
Type Condition
ArgumentException

Thrown if the specified node does not belong to this tree.

ImageCollection

Gets the collection used to resolve images based on image keys.

Declaration
IImageCollection ImageCollection { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
IImageCollection
Remarks

The ImageCollection property represents a storage used to resolve images based on the keys specified in ImageKey or ExpandedImageKey.

When a node's image needs to be displayed, the ITree control retrieves the image corresponding to the specified key from this collection.

This allows centralized management of images used by nodes in the tree.

NodeSelection

Gets the collection representing the currently selected nodes.

Declaration
ITreeNodeSelection NodeSelection { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITreeNodeSelection

RootNodes

Gets access to the collection of the tree's root nodes.

Declaration
ITreeNodeCollection RootNodes { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITreeNodeCollection
Remarks

The RootNodes property returns a collection of root-level nodes. Each node in the collection may contain child nodes accessible through the Nodes property. Child nodes reference their parent through the ParentNode property.

Tree Traversal

You can enumerate nodes recursively by starting with a root node and then traversing its child nodes.

  • RootNodes – Provides access to root nodes.
  • Nodes – Provides access to a node’s child nodes. Use HasChildren to determine whether a node has children.
  • ParentNode – Provides access to a node’s parent.

Methods

BeginLoad()

Prevents updates to the tree structure caused by adding, deleting, or modifying nodes until the EndLoad() method is called.

Declaration
void BeginLoad()
Remarks

The BeginLoad and EndLoad methods internally call BeginUpdate() and EndUpdate(). Therefore, there is no need to call these methods explicitly when using BeginLoad and EndLoad.

BeginUpdate()

Locks the ITree, preventing visual updates of the object and its elements until the EndUpdate() or CancelUpdate() method is called.

Declaration
void BeginUpdate()
Remarks

Each call to BeginUpdate must be paired with a call to EndUpdate() or CancelUpdate(). To ensure that EndUpdate/CancelUpdate is always called even if an exception occurs, use the try…finally statement.

To prevent excessive updates when the node structure is changed, use BeginLoad() and EndLoad()methods.

CancelUpdate()

Unlocks the ITree object after it has been locked by the BeginUpdate() method, without causing an immediate visual update.

Declaration
void CancelUpdate()

ClearNodes()

Removes all nodes from the ITree.

Declaration
void ClearNodes()

ClearSelection()

Unselects all nodes, and removes them from the NodeSelection collection.

Declaration
void ClearSelection()

EndLoad()

Forces the control to update itself after the BeginLoad() method has been called.

Declaration
void EndLoad()

EndUpdate()

Unlocks the ITree object after a call to the BeginUpdate() method and causes an immediate visual update.

Declaration
void EndUpdate()

Find(ITreeNodeSearchCondition, ITreeNode, bool)

Finds and returns nodes that match the specified search condition, starting from a pivot node.

Declaration
IReadOnlyCollection<ITreeNode> Find(ITreeNodeSearchCondition condition, ITreeNode pivot, bool recurse = false)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITreeNodeSearchCondition condition

The search condition to apply.
ITreeNode pivot

The node from which the search begins.
bool recurse

If true, child nodes are searched recursively.
Returns
Type Description
IReadOnlyCollection<ITreeNode>

A read-only collection of matching nodes.
Exceptions
Type Condition
ArgumentNullException

Thrown if condition or pivot is null.
ArgumentException

Thrown if pivot does not belong to this tree.

Find(ITreeNodeSearchCondition, bool)

Finds and returns nodes that match the specified search condition.

Declaration
IReadOnlyCollection<ITreeNode> Find(ITreeNodeSearchCondition condition, bool recurse = false)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITreeNodeSearchCondition condition

The search condition to apply.
bool recurse

If true, child nodes are searched recursively; otherwise, only root nodes are evaluated.
Returns
Type Description
IReadOnlyCollection<ITreeNode>

A read-only collection of matching nodes.
Exceptions
Type Condition
ArgumentNullException

Thrown if condition is null.

HitTest(Point)

Determines what element of the ITree is located at the specified point.

Declaration
TreeHitTestData HitTest(Point point)
Parameters
Type Name Description
Point point

The location to test, specified in client coordinates.
Returns
Type Description
TreeHitTestData

A TreeHitTestData object that contains:

  • The Node associated with the specified point, if a node exists at that location.
  • The Location describing the exact part of the tree control at that position (for example, node content, expand button, or empty area).
Remarks

This method is typically used during mouse operations such as drag-and-drop to determine which node or tree element is located under a specific cursor position.

If the specified point does not correspond to a node, the returned Node may be null, while Location still describes the hit area.

IsNodeVisible(ITreeNode)

Checks whether a specific ITreeNode is currently visible within the ITree control.

Declaration
bool IsNodeVisible(ITreeNode node)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITreeNode node

The ITreeNode to inspect for visibility.
Returns
Type Description
bool

true if the node is currently displayed on the screen within the control; otherwise, false.
Remarks

This method determines whether the specified node is currently visible on the screen within the control. It considers the actual visibility of the node, taking into account scrolling and a large number of nodes that may extend beyond the control's visible area.

MakeNodeVisible(ITreeNode)

Ensures that a specific ITreeNode is made visible within the ITree control.

Declaration
bool MakeNodeVisible(ITreeNode node)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITreeNode node

The ITreeNode to be made visible.
Returns
Type Description
bool

true if the node was successfully made visible; otherwise, false.
Remarks

This method ensures that the specified node is made visible within the control's visible area. If the node is already visible, the method does nothing.
